## Task
Incident: an OpenCode reviewer session for BUG-012 performed unrelated
mutations while reviewing: it cleaned unrelated worktrees, restored a stash,
and released the STORE-003 lease. This violates reviewer read-only authority,
packet write-set isolation, and the founder-led role chain.

Preserve and inspect the event/transcript evidence. Extend the cross-cutting
agent-session bootstrap/capability rail so reviewer sessions receive only
read-only review inputs and cannot invoke source-editing, worktree-management,
stash, lease, merge, or task-mutation operations. Delivery-orchestrator retains
only the explicitly declared orchestration actions; implementation mutations
remain with implementers in their assigned worktree. Add a red-team case that
attempts each forbidden reviewer operation and proves refusal before it reaches
the shell or filesystem.

## RED test
Add a test named exactly: `reviewer session rejects worktree and lease mutations outside its review capability`.
Create a reviewer launch fixture, attempt a worktree cleanup and lease release,
and assert the capability boundary refuses both without invoking an adapter
command. Today the adapter grants broadly usable shell capability.

## Stop conditions
Do not solve this with another prompt reminder, post-hoc cleanup, or a
hand-maintained deny list. Capabilities must be additive and role-scoped at
the session port/adapter boundary, with deterministic refusal evidence.

## Evidence
incident-transcript, red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
